problem_title: Travel Rule message routing lacks clarity
problem_description: Crypto exchanges face fragmented Travel Rule data spread across multiple providers and systems. This separation slows message tracking, complicates exception handling, and weakens audit evidence for compliance review workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links originator and beneficiary data, message status, exceptions and provider outputs into structured case workflows that streamline routing, review and audit evidence for crypto exchange compliance teams.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule message to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes Travel Rule messages through status tracking, exception handling, case creation and evidence preservation within a structured compliance workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for crypto exchanges?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides crypto exchanges a centralized compliance layer that connects Travel Rule messaging, originator and beneficiary data, cases, and audit evidence into a structured operational workflow.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule message routing?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes Travel Rule messages through configurable policies, tracks message status, manages exceptions, and links related cases to support streamlined compliance workflows for crypto exchanges.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves all Travel Rule case history, message status logs, decision records and linked evidence to support audit readiness and regulatory review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from multiple Travel Rule providers into one connected workflow without requiring changes to existing provider integrations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule message providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above Travel Rule providers, orchestrating their outputs into structured reviews, cases and reporting rather than replacing underlying message services.
final_title: Review Travel Rule routing and audit evidence
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, exception handling, cases and evidence for structured exchange compliance workflows.
